account.disclaimer: Signing in protects your account identity and prepares future cloud sync. Link Apple and Google to the same account. Workouts stay on this device for now.
account.linkedFooter: Signing out or deleting your account does not erase workouts saved on this device. Remote sign-out ends that device’s session when it next syncs.
account.title: Account
balanced.title: Balanced
calendar.selectDayHint: Tap a day to see workouts.

exercise.name: Exercise Name
exercise.removeConfirmMessage: This exercise has logged sets. Are you sure you want to remove it from the workout?
exercise.removeLoggedMessage: Some exercises you unchecked have logged sets. Remove them from the workout?

recycleBin.footer: Workouts are permanently removed after 30 days.
recycleBin.pendingFlash: Queued to continue after your current workout
recycleBin.title: Recycle Bin
set.deleteConfirmMessage: This set has logged values. Are you sure you want to delete it?
subgroup.empty: No exercises in this subgroup yet.
subgroup.formFooter: Choose a primary muscle subgroup so the picker can suggest related lifts.
subgroup.sectionFooter: Filter by region. All shows every exercise in this group.
superset.defaultReadOnlyFooter: Customize to edit this default. After saving, it moves to My Supersets.
superset.deleteBuiltInMessage: This permanently removes the default preset. It will not come back after updates. Past workouts stay unchanged.
superset.deleteMessage: This removes the saved preset only. Past workouts stay unchanged.
superset.groupFooter: Do these back-to-back without resting between exercises.
superset.libraryEmptyDescription: Save a reusable pair or trio of exercises for one-tap add.

superset.mineEmpty: No custom supersets yet. Create one or customize a default.

superset.removeAllMessage: All exercises and sets in this superset will be removed from the workout.
superset.resetMessage: Your name, exercise, and order changes will be removed. The original preset will return to Defaults.
superset.selectMembersHint: Select at least two exercises to link as a superset.
sync.footer: Finished workouts sync by month while signed in. Resync refreshes months already loaded; use Load this year or Load all years for older cloud history. In-progress sessions stay on this device until you finish.

template.noExercises: No exercises yet. Tap Edit to add some.
workout.discardMessage: This in-progress workout will move to Recycle Bin for 30 days.

workout.noExercisesYet: No exercises yet. Add one to start logging sets.
workout.reorderFooter: Drag to change order. Logged sets stay with each exercise.
workout.seeAllTemplatesHint: See all templates on the Templates tab.
workout.staleFinishMessage: This workout has been inactive for a while. Choose how many minutes to add after the last activity.
account.adoptExisting.message: This Apple or Google sign-in already belongs to another account. Continuing switches to that account. Your guest cloud profile on this device will be abandoned. Workouts stay on this device.
account.adoptExisting.title: Use existing account?
account.delete.confirmMessage: Removes your Firebase account. Workouts on this device are kept.
account.delete.reauthMessage: Sign in again with Apple or Google to delete your account.
account.devices.footer: Up to 10 recent devices. Sign out a device remotely; it leaves the account when it next opens the app.
account.devices.revokeMessage: That device will return to a guest account when it next syncs. Workouts on that device stay there.
account.error.accountExists: This sign-in is already linked to another account. Stay as guest, or continue to that account.
account.error.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
account.error.network: Network unavailable. Try again when you’re online.
account.error.requiresRecentLogin: Please sign in again to continue.
account.legal.footer: By continuing, you agree to the Terms and Conditions and Privacy Policy.
account.legal.loadError: Couldn’t load this page. Check your connection and try again.
account.measurement.footer: Applies to exercises without their own unit override. History values stay stored in kg/meters.
account.measurement.title: Units
account.prompt.insightsBody: You’re using a guest account. Sign in so your identity is ready for future sync — workouts are not uploaded yet.
account.prompt.workoutBody: Link Apple or Google so your account is ready when cloud sync arrives. Workouts stay local for now.
account.prompt.workoutTitle: Sign in to keep your progress
account.providers.footer: Add Apple and Google so you can sign in with either on another device. Same account — workouts still stay local until cloud sync ships.
account.signOut.confirmMessage: You’ll return to a guest account. Workouts on this device stay here.
account.status.guestSubtitle: You can log workouts now. Sign in when you want a lasting account.
balanced.range.footer: Balance uses finished workouts in this window.
balanced.spider.empty: No finished workouts in this window yet. Pick muscle groups below — we will still suggest exercises.
balanced.spider.footer: Tap a spoke to add or remove that muscle group for today.
balanced.suggestions.empty: No matching exercises for the selected groups.
balanced.suggestions.pickGroups: Select at least one muscle group to see suggestions.
balanced.trainToday.footer: Under-trained groups are preselected from your activity. Change freely.
e1rm.info.message:
e1RM estimates the maximum weight you could lift once using the Epley formula:

weight × (1 + reps ÷ 30)

For a 1-rep set, e1RM equals the lifted weight. The app keeps the highest estimate from your performed weight-and-reps sets. It is an estimate, not a tested maximum.
e1rm.info.title: How e1RM Works

gate.maintenance.message: Gym Logbook is undergoing maintenance. Please try again shortly.

healthKit.settings.denied: Health access was denied. Enable Gym Logbook in the Health app to sync workouts.
healthKit.settings.failedCount: %lld workouts failed to sync. Use Sync recent or Retry on a workout.
healthKit.settings.footer: Finished workouts are saved to Apple Health as workout time only. Calories are never estimated or written.

healthKit.settings.unavailable: Apple Health is not available on this device.
healthKit.settings.upToDate: Workouts are up to date in Apple Health.
import.conflict.intro: Some imported workouts share the same start time as workouts already on this device. Choose what to keep for each.
import.conflict.title: Resolve Conflicts
import.error.title: Import Failed
import.result.message:
Workouts added: %lld, updated: %lld, skipped: %lld.
Exercises: %lld · Templates: %lld · PRs: %lld · Supersets: %lld

language.conflict.message: iOS Settings language is now %2$@, but this app is set to %1$@. Which language do you want to use?
language.conflict.title: Language changed
onboarding.apply.body: When you have history, the app suggests the next load. Tap Apply to fill an empty set.
onboarding.apply.footer: Apply disappears after you use it so the logger stays clean.
onboarding.apply.hint: Suggested: 62.5 — you hit all reps last time
onboarding.apply.title: Build on last time
onboarding.balanced.body: Balanced opens from the Workout tab. Pick a lookback, see muscle balance, choose groups, and start with suggested lifts.
onboarding.balanced.step2: 2. Review the balance spider; under-trained groups are preselected
onboarding.balanced.step3: 3. Confirm exercises and start — one blank set each
onboarding.balanced.title: Train what you've neglected
onboarding.categories.body: Each muscle group has a soft color so you can recognize workout types throughout the app.
onboarding.categories.title: Categories have colors
onboarding.exercise.body: Tap through the steps. Muscle roles power Insights and balance suggestions.
onboarding.exercise.catalogNote: Most lifts are already in the catalog — create custom only when you need something new.
onboarding.exercise.title: Creating a custom exercise
onboarding.exercise.why: Choosing muscles keeps category Insights and balance suggestions accurate.
onboarding.favorites.body: Star lifts you use often. Tap ⓘ next to a name for form cues and target muscle maps.
onboarding.favorites.info: Form guide — art, cues, target regions
onboarding.favorites.star: Favorite — appears first in the picker
onboarding.favorites.title: Star favorites & form guides
onboarding.gradients.benchMeta: Chest · Shoulders · Arms · Weight and reps
onboarding.gradients.body: Exercise cards blend primary, secondary, and tertiary muscle colors.
onboarding.gradients.supersetBody: Supersets combine member muscle colors into one soft multi-stop accent.
onboarding.gradients.supersetCaption: Up to three muscle hues — the workout’s overall focus.
onboarding.gradients.title: Gradients show involved muscles
onboarding.healthKitDemo.body: Connect Gym Logbook to the Apple Health app so finished strength and cardio sessions appear there.
onboarding.healthKitDemo.connectA11y: Connect Gym Logbook to the Apple Health app
onboarding.healthKitDemo.privacy: Optional. Requests write access only — it does not read Health data.
onboarding.healthKitDemo.syncRecent: Sync Recent can add up to 30 finished workouts
onboarding.healthKitDemo.title: Connect to Apple Health
onboarding.historyDemo.body: The History tab is a calendar of trained days. Search finds workouts by exercise name in any language.
onboarding.historyDemo.search: Search by exercise from the History search bar
onboarding.historyDemo.title: History at a glance
onboarding.insights.body: Insights, Records, and progress charts turn your log into motivation — not noise.
onboarding.insights.exportTip: Backup anytime from Insights → Export (JSON or CSV).
onboarding.insights.prDetail: Quiet celebration when you set a best
onboarding.insights.title: See why consistency pays off
onboarding.language.body: The app and exercise names follow this language.
onboarding.language.footnote: You can change your language anytime in iOS Settings.
onboarding.language.title: Choose your language
onboarding.layout.body: Pick List for a fast scan, or Cards for form art and muscle cues.
onboarding.layout.cardsHint: Cards help you recognize lifts by illustration and muscle colors.
onboarding.layout.footnote: You can switch layouts anytime from the exercise picker.
onboarding.layout.listHint: List is great when you already know the name and want fewer taps.
onboarding.layout.title: Choose how exercises look
onboarding.library.intro: Jump to any topic, or replay a short session.
onboarding.library.title: App Tour
onboarding.logging.body: Tap weight or reps to open the wheel picker. Swipe a set left to delete. Tap another exercise to expand it — only one stays open.
onboarding.logging.collapseTip: Only one exercise stays expanded so you can scan what’s done and what’s left.
onboarding.logging.collapsedExpandedHint: Log sets here when this exercise is open. Tap another title to switch.
onboarding.logging.swipeTip: Swipe a set left to delete — same as during a workout.
onboarding.logging.title: Log sets in seconds
onboarding.logging.why: Consistent logging unlocks progress charts, Insights, and personal records.
onboarding.measurement.body: Choose how weights and distances are shown. You can change this later in Account, and override it per exercise while logging.
onboarding.measurement.footnote: Suggested from your region. Default if unset is Metric.
onboarding.measurement.title: Measurement System
onboarding.nudge2.body: A short tour of history, Apply, Balanced workouts, and templates — about a minute.
onboarding.nudge2.title: Ready for the next tips?
onboarding.nudge3.body: Recycle Bin, rest timer, Insights, and advanced picker tips — skip anytime.
onboarding.nudge3.title: A few power tips
onboarding.personal.body: A few preferences stay under your control — here’s where they live.
onboarding.personal.exercisesDetail: Templates → Exercises to add, edit, and favorite.
onboarding.personal.footnote: These options stay available as you train — no extra setup required.
onboarding.personal.languageDetail: Chosen here; also follows iOS Settings for the system language.
onboarding.personal.layoutDetail: Toggle anytime in the exercise picker toolbar.
onboarding.personal.measurementDetail: Metric or Imperial — Account and per-exercise override
onboarding.personal.supersetsDetail: Library presets and your custom groups under Templates → Exercises.
onboarding.personal.themeDetail: Light and Dark follow your iPhone appearance setting.
onboarding.personal.title: Make it yours
onboarding.ready.body: Start logging your first workout — or explore the tabs at your own pace.
onboarding.ready.title: You’re ready
onboarding.recycle.body: Deleting a finished workout or discarding an in-progress one moves it to the Recycle Bin.
onboarding.recycle.bullet2: Continue an unfinished session you discarded
onboarding.recycle.bullet3: PRs ignore trashed workouts until restored
onboarding.recycle.title: Nothing is gone forever — for 30 days
onboarding.rest.body: On an expanded exercise, start or stop rest manually. Timing also shows on the Lock Screen and Dynamic Island.
onboarding.rest.liveActivity: Live Activity keeps rest visible while you rack the bar
onboarding.rest.title: Rest between sets
onboarding.rest.where: Active workout · under Add Set / Dup
onboarding.session1.title: Core tour
onboarding.session2.title: Progress & balance
onboarding.session3.title: Power tips
onboarding.skip.hint: Skip onboarding and open the app
onboarding.superset.alternating: Alternating focus: A1 → B1 → C1 → A2 → B2 → C2
onboarding.superset.body: Group exercises, expand a preset, and alternate focus A → B → C while you train.
onboarding.superset.title: Supersets keep complex work organized
onboarding.superset.why: Supersets help organize complex workouts and keep paired lifts together.
onboarding.templates.autofill: Templates live on the Templates tab. Create opens the exercise picker automatically.
onboarding.templates.body: Build a template once. Starting it auto-fills planned sets so you can train faster.
onboarding.templates.title: Save routines as templates
onboarding.welcome.body: Organize workouts, build routines, track progress, monitor muscle balance, and see how you improve over time.
onboarding.welcome.title: Your weightlifting logbook
